description_long: This object is a tall, rectangular column with a fluted shaft, reminiscent of classical architectural obelisks. The shaft is vertically grooved, giving it a ribbed texture, and is topped with a flat, square capital that is slightly wider than the shaft itself. The base is also square and appears solid, providing stability and a sense of monumentality. The entire structure is rendered in shades of gray, suggesting it is made from stone or concrete. There are no visible inscriptions or decorative motifs, giving it a minimalist and utilitarian appearance. The overall design is simple, functional, and evokes the look of ancient Greco-Roman columns or modern urban monuments.
description: A tall, fluted gray column with a square base and capital, resembling a minimalist stone obelisk. The surface is ribbed and unadorned, with a solid, sturdy appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the fluted shaft, square base, and capital, with all grooves clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, with the same fluted pattern and square elements visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the same vertical grooves and square base and top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the fluted shaft and square features, with no additional details.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple, solid square platform, slightly wider than the shaft, with no visible steps or decorative features. It appears robust and functional, likely made of the same material as the shaft.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is a flat, square capital with no ornamentation or unique features, matching the minimalist style of the rest of the structure.
